touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn outreach to Dakota Campbell at ScaleCo regarding pipeline visibility challenges. No response.
Cold call attempt to Dakota Campbell. Left voicemail about improving CS team's pipeline visibility.
Follow-up cold call attempt. No answer, no voicemail left this time.
Second LinkedIn message referencing previous voicemail and offering brief demo. No response.
Cold call attempt. Reached assistant who noted Dakota was in meetings all week.
Final cold call attempt before pause. Left voicemail mentioning ScaleCo's recent growth.
Brief connection call. Dakota confirmed interest in pipeline visibility tools for CS team. Currently frustrated with manual reporting. Agreed to longer qualification call.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your interest in pipeline visibility right now?
questions_asked.answer: Our CS team is spending hours each week manually pulling data from multiple sources. I need better visibility into renewal risk.

next_steps: Schedule 30-min qualification call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call covered current tech stack, team size (9 CS reps), and pain points around manual reporting. Dakota mentioned they're also looking at Gong but likes our automation focus. Budget appears flexible for right solution.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently tracking customer health and renewal pipeline?
questions_asked.answer: Spreadsheets mostly. We pull data from Salesforce and our support tool manually. It's very time-consuming and often out of date.

questions_asked.question: What tools are you evaluating besides us?
questions_asked.answer: We've had a demo with Gong. They're strong on call analytics but seem expensive for what we need. I'm more interested in automation.

questions_asked.question: Can your system integrate with our existing Salesforce instance?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration. Most customers are up and running within a week.

objections: Concerned about implementation timeline - need something fast

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with technical docs, integration guide, and proposed demo agenda. Dakota responded same day confirming demo time and asking to include two CS managers.

questions_asked.question: Can we include Sarah and Mike from my CS management team on the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, the more stakeholders we can include the better. I'll send calendar invites to all three of you.

touches.ai_summary: Technical demo with Dakota and two CS managers. Team engaged with automation features and asked detailed questions about reporting customization. Dakota mentioned budget constraints but seemed motivated. Only Dakota and CS managers present - no exec sponsor involved yet.

questions_asked.question: How customizable are the automated reports?
questions_asked.answer: Fully customizable. You can set triggers, thresholds, and create custom views for different stakeholders.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical contract length and pricing structure?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers start with annual contracts. Pricing is per-seat with unlimited automation on our professional tier.

questions_asked.question: Would it make sense to loop in your CFO or VP of Operations on pricing discussions?
questions_asked.answer: I have budget authority for tools under 15K. Let me get through internal discussions with my team first.

objections: Pricing seems higher than Gong's quote for similar functionality
next_steps: Dakota to review internally with CS team

touches.ai_summary: Meeting labeled 'exec meeting' but only Dakota attended - no executive sponsor present. Focused heavily on pricing negotiation. Dakota pushed for significant discount citing Gong's competitive offer. Agreed to 28.9% discount to close deal by month-end. Still no multi-threading beyond CS team.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Gong's pricing? They came in about 30% lower.
questions_asked.answer: Let me see what I can do. Given your team size and commitment to annual contract, I can offer 28.9% off list price.

questions_asked.question: Before we finalize, should we schedule a brief call with your VP of Operations to ensure alignment?
questions_asked.answer: Not necessary - I'm the decision maker for CS tooling. Let's move forward with the discounted pricing.

questions_asked.question: When can we start implementation if we sign this week?
questions_asked.answer: Implementation team can start within 3-5 business days of contract signature.

objections: Still comparing against Gong's lower price point
Needs to close by end of quarter for budget reasons

touches.ai_summary: Contract sent with 28.9% discount. Dakota confirmed received and will route to legal for review. Expects to sign within 48 hours. Brief email exchange - Dakota seems motivated to close quickly but still single-threaded through CS department only.
next_steps: Target signature by end of week
